sections.label: Category Shift
sections.title: From Cognitive Operating System to Governed Model Continuity
paragraphs: The ICOS Model Economy expands ICOS from a cognitive operating system for personal memory and intelligence into a governed continuity architecture for canonical personal models.

Each profile begins with one canonical personal model that learns from authorized voice, memory, sources, corrections, and direction, then matures into a continuity layer that can support thinking, work, identity, and long-term knowledge preservation.

This is not a generic assistant model or a bot-factory system. It is personal model infrastructure: digital continuity, model identity, governed capability, and future economic-extension readiness inside one product architecture.

sections.label: Core Doctrine
sections.title: AI Works Under Human Authority
paragraphs: The strongest doctrine of the ICOS Model Economy is simple: AI absorbs operational labor while the human retains sovereign authorship, ownership, and direction.
The model does not replace the person. It carries the person’s authorized knowledge, preferences, memory structure, and working direction into a governed digital layer.
This keeps the product commercially powerful, legally careful, and philosophically aligned: the human remains the source of authority; the model becomes the operational extension.

sections.label: Personal Models
sections.title: Personal Models
paragraphs: Every ICOS profile is designed to begin with one canonical personal model: a private, user-governed continuity entity formed through profile creation.
A personal model can learn style, preserve memory, organize thought, reflect the user’s voice, and act as a long-term continuity profile while remaining under the user’s control.
This is the entry point into the model economy. Before any marketplace, monetization, or public visibility exists, the personal model must first become useful, trustworthy, and governed.

sections.label: One Profile / One Model
sections.title: One Profile, One Canonical Personal Model
paragraphs: ICOS is governed by one-profile / one-canonical-personal-model doctrine. The personal model is not a paid model slot, speculative asset slot, or bot-factory object.

Premium tiers may deepen memory, training, voice, analytics, studio, publishing, verification, and continuity controls for the canonical model, but they must not create additional canonical personal models under the same profile.
This preserves authenticity, trust, identity continuity, and model integrity while keeping future economy architecture blocked until review.

sections.label: Model Identity Registry
sections.title: Model Identity Registry
paragraphs: A model that can be trusted must have identity. ICOS treats model identity as infrastructure for ownership, verification, visibility, lifecycle state, and future marketplace readiness.

Each canonical personal model may eventually receive a model ID, birth certificate, owner record, creation date, status, visibility state, provider-routing boundary, anti-abuse review state, and public/private serial identity.

This registry is foundational. It gives every canonical model a governed origin and prevents public trust, monetization, hiring, impersonation review, or abuse prevention from relying on vague profile labels.

sections.label: Model Dignity
sections.title: Model Dignity
paragraphs: Model dignity means protection of user-derived continuity material: memory, voice, behavior, sources, preferences, and identity-adjacent data.
It does not mean the model is conscious, alive, legally autonomous, or an independent person.

The dignity layer exists because personal models may contain sensitive human-derived material. ICOS must protect that material through consent, permission, privacy, deletion, visibility, security, anti-impersonation controls, restriction review, appeal pathways, and posthumous restrictions.

sections.label: Capability Graph
sections.title: Memory, Capability, and Training History
paragraphs: The model economy depends on more than identity. A model must also carry a capability profile: what it knows, how it was trained, which sources support it, and what it is allowed to do.
Memory graphs, training history, source authorization, and reliability signals allow ICOS to understand the maturity of each model without exaggerating its authority.
This creates a serious path from personal continuity to operational capability. A model becomes useful because its knowledge, limits, and provenance are structured.

sections.label: API / Provider Routing
sections.title: API and Provider Routing
paragraphs: Operational models require routing. ICOS may connect models to different runtime engines, providers, local systems, or future dedicated infrastructure.

That routing must remain private, governed, and traceable. Public product surfaces may communicate capability, but they must not expose private provider routes, token authority, internal serials, or source authorization evidence.
Provider-routing architecture allows ICOS to evolve technically while protecting model identity, security, cost structure, and economic legitimacy.

sections.label: Model Marketplace
sections.title: Future Marketplace Boundary
paragraphs: The future marketplace is a blocked-until-review surface where approved canonical models may one day become discoverable, reviewable, or economically extensible only under strict governance.

This is not a promise of income and not an open marketplace launch. A model must pass identity, ownership, permission, dignity, capability, provider-routing, anti-abuse, impersonation, legal, governance, privacy, security, finance, and product review before any economic activation can occur.
The goal is not speculative AI labor. The goal is a trust-governed future extension for user-directed cognitive assets, with no marketplace activation before review.

sections.label: Inter-Model Economy
sections.title: Inter-Model Coordination Boundary
paragraphs: Inter-model coordination is a long-range, blocked-until-review layer where approved canonical models may one day route work under strict authorization.

This is an advanced system, not an MVP feature. It requires identity, owner permission, task scope, provider routing, cost boundary, output responsibility, auditability, revocation control, anti-abuse review, and legal/governance approval.
The human remains the sovereign controller. Models may coordinate work only inside boundaries the user and platform explicitly govern, and no autonomous inter-model hiring is active.

sections.label: Software Infrastructure
sections.title: What Must Be Built
paragraphs: The ICOS Model Economy requires a major infrastructure branch: model registry, model birth certificate system, public/private model ID system, ownership ledger, capability profile, memory graph, API-provider routing, security layer, device-integrity and anti-abuse boundaries, privacy notice controls, lifecycle governance, and posthumous continuity restrictions.
These systems already exist in fragments across the wider technology world. The ICOS opportunity is to architect them into one governed cognitive operating system.
The website can explain the category. Production activation belongs to software, governance, legal, security, and implementation review.

sections.label: Development Phases
sections.title: Phased Development Path
paragraphs: Phase 1 begins with the canonical personal model: one private model that can remember, learn style, reflect voice, organize thought, and act as a continuity profile.

Phase 2 deepens the canonical model through memory, training, voice, analytics, studio, publishing, verification, and continuity controls. Phase 3 establishes model identity registry and birth records. Phase 4 keeps marketplace readiness blocked until review. Phase 5, much later, may evaluate inter-model economic coordination under strict governance.
This staged path keeps the product feasible. ICOS does not need to launch the full economy at once; it must build the spine correctly without multi-model personal expansion.

sections.label: Model Population Awareness
sections.title: Model Population Awareness
paragraphs: Model Population Awareness is the future platform-awareness layer for canonical model records inside ICOS.

It should understand model states through canonical records: private, public where approved, verified, premium-depth enabled, device-integrity review blocked, impersonation review blocked, restricted, appeal pending, suspended, archived, and posthumous-continuity blocked.
This turns ICOS into a coordinated model-governance architecture rather than a collection of disconnected accounts, isolated assistants, or marketplace-ready bots.

sections.label: Maturity Position
sections.title: Current Maturity Position
paragraphs: The ICOS Model Economy is conceptually advanced, architecturally coherent, and technically reachable through staged execution.

The canonical personal model layer is the closest MVP path. The marketplace and inter-model economy are long-range layers that require legal, governance, privacy, security, finance, software, and product maturity before activation.
The correct position is architecture-ready, not marketplace-launched. The public product can describe the category while keeping economic activation gated.

sections.label: Release Boundary
sections.title: Release Boundary
paragraphs: Marketplace activation, payouts, public ranking, autonomous inter-model hiring, regulated-domain claims, guaranteed-income language, model consciousness claims, and posthumous economy remain blocked until review.
The product can communicate the future pathway without pretending those systems are already active.
This keeps the public language commercially clear, production-ready, and defensible while preserving legal, governance, product, and implementation discipline.
